title: Marketing Freedom for Grain Farmers Act

short_name: Marketing Freedom for Grain Farmers Act
description: Marketing Freedom for Grain Farmers Act (Marketing Freedom for Grain Farmers Act). Current to 2026-02-04.

provisions.title: Short title
provisions.content: 1 This Act may be cited as the Marketing Freedom for Grain Farmers Act .

provisions.chapter: Amendments to the Canadian Wheat Board Act
Enactment of Canadian Wheat Board (Interim Operations) Act
provisions.title: Enactment
provisions.content: 14 The Canadian Wheat Board (Interim Operations) Act is enacted as follows: [See Canadian Wheat Board (Interim Operations) Act ]

provisions.title: Enactment
provisions.content: 22 [Amendment] An Act to amend the Canada Grain Act and the Agriculture and Agri-Food Administrative Monetary Penalties Act and to repeal the Grain Futures Act

provisions.title: Order in council
provisions.content: 40 This Part comes into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council. Return to footnote * [Note: Part 2 in force August 1, 2012, see SI/2011-120.]

provisions.title: Definitions
provisions.content: 41 (1) The following definitions apply in this Part and in Parts 4 and 5. board board means the board of directors of the Corporation as defined in subection 2(1) of the Canadian Wheat Board (Interim Operations) Act . ( conseil ) Corporation Corporation means the Canadian Wheat Board continued by subsection 4(1) of the Canadian Wheat Board (Interim Operations) Act . ( Commission ) Minister Minister means the Minister of Agriculture and Agri-Food. ( ministre ) (2) Unless it is otherwise provided, words and expressions used in this Part or in Part 4 or 5 have the same meaning as in the Canadian Wheat Board (Interim Operations) Act .

provisions.title: Submission to Minister
provisions.content: 42 (1) The Corporation must submit an application for continuance under one of the following Acts for the Minister’s approval: (a) the Canada Business Corporations Act ; (b) the Canada Cooperatives Act ; or (c) the Canada Not-for-profit Corporations Act . (2) The application must be submitted to the Minister within four years, or any shorter period specified by the Minister, after the day on which this Part comes into force. (3) Once the application has been approved by the Minister, the Corporation must submit the application to the applicable authorities under the relevant Act. (4) The application is not invalid solely because the Corporation is incorporated by an Act of Parliament.

provisions.title: Restriction
provisions.content: 43 The Corporation is not entitled to apply for continuance in another jurisdiction.

provisions.title: Simultaneous coming into force
provisions.content: 44 This Part comes into force on the day on which Part 2 comes into force. Return to footnote * [Note: Part 3 in force August 1, 2012, see SI/2011-120.]

provisions.title: Ceasing to have effect
provisions.content: 45 This Part ceases to have effect on the day on which Part 4 applies.

provisions.title: Application of Part
provisions.content: 46 This Part applies only if the Corporation is not continued under Part 3 within five years, or any shorter period specified by the Governor in Council, after the day on which that Part comes into force.

provisions.title: Final pool period
provisions.content: 47 (1) The Minister must, by order, in respect of each grain, designate one or more pool periods set by the Corporation under section 27 of the Canadian Wheat Board (Interim Operations) Act as a final pool period for the purposes of winding up the Corporation. (2) The Corporation must make all payments due under subsection 29(3) of the Canadian Wheat Board (Interim Operations) Act in respect of the final pool period before winding up the Corporation under section 48. (3) After the end of the final pool period, the Corporation may exercise its powers under the Canadian Wheat Board (Interim Operations) Act only for the purpose of administering that pool period and winding up the Corporation.

provisions.title: Distribution of property
provisions.content: 48 (1) The property of the Corporation is to be applied in satisfaction of its debts and liabilities, and the charges, costs and expenses incurred in winding-up its affairs. (2) For greater certainty, the contingency fund established under subsection 18(1) of the Canadian Wheat Board (Interim Operations) Act forms part of the property of the Corporation.

provisions.title: Deadline for claims
provisions.content: 49 (1) The Minister may fix a date by which all claims against the Corporation are to be submitted to the Corporation and must publish a notice of that date in the Canada Gazette at least 60 days before that date. (2) Any claim against the Corporation that is not submitted in accordance with subsection (1) is void.

provisions.title: Winding-up expenses
provisions.content: 50 All charges, costs and expenses properly incurred in the winding-up of the Corporation, including the remuneration of any liquidator appointed under section 52, are payable out of the assets of the Corporation, in priority to all other claims.

provisions.title: Surplus
provisions.content: 51 (1) Any surplus that remains after the satisfaction of the debts and liabilities of the Corporation and the winding-up charges, costs and expenses belongs to Her Majesty in right of Canada. (2) Any debts and liabilities of the Corporation that remain unsatisfied on the day on which the Corporation is dissolved become the debts and liabilities of Her Majesty in right of Canada.

provisions.title: Appointment
provisions.content: 52 If the Minister considers it necessary to fully carry out the purpose of this Part, the Minister may appoint a liquidator, who is to hold office during pleasure, to administer the final pool period and wind up the Corporation.

provisions.title: Powers, duties and functions
provisions.content: 53 On appointment of a liquidator, (a) the president, chairperson and other directors cease to hold their respective offices and the liquidator may exercise all of the powers and perform all the duties and functions of the Corporation; and (b) the by-laws made under section 9 of the Canadian Wheat Board (Interim Operations) Act are deemed to be revoked.

provisions.title: Refusal to honour
provisions.content: 54 The liquidator may refuse to honour any gratuitous contract, or conveyance or contract without consideration or with a merely nominal consideration, respecting either real or personal property or immovables or movables, made by the Corporation before the appointment of the liquidator.

provisions.title: Dissolution
provisions.content: 55 The Corporation is dissolved on a day fixed by order of the Governor in Council.

provisions.title: Application of Part
provisions.content: 56 This Part applies when the Corporation is continued under Part 3 or dissolved under Part 4.

provisions.title: Library and Archives of Canada Act
provisions.content: 57 Despite sections 58 and 63, the Library and Archives of Canada Act continues to apply to the Corporation but only in respect of government records, as defined in that Act, that were under the control of the Corporation on the day before the day on which this Part applies.

definitions.definition: board means the board of directors of the Corporation as defined in subection 2(1) of the Canadian Wheat Board (Interim Operations) Act . ( conseil )
Corporation means the Canadian Wheat Board continued by subsection 4(1) of the Canadian Wheat Board (Interim Operations) Act . ( Commission )
Minister means the Minister of Agriculture and Agri-Food. ( ministre )
